doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_sqr_2 (mpfr/src/sqr.c L232-L356). Two-limb square fast path: GMP_NUMB_BITS < prec(b) <= 2*GMP_NUMB_BITS AND prec(a) == prec(b). Algorithm: compute the 4-limb product b^2 = (b1*B + b0)^2 = b1^2 * B^2 + 2*b1*b0 * B + b0^2 via three umul_ppmm calls; assemble the upper 2 limbs (h, l) plus carry into sb=high(b0*c0); use the approximate-rounding trick when the low bits of l are not all-0/all-1/all-2 (faster path that skips the b0^2 limb), else compute it fully; on a0 < HIGHBIT, shift left by 1 and decrement ax; extract round bit rb = bit (sh-1) of l, sticky sb |= (l & mask) ^ rb | sb2; apply 5-mode rounding. The TS port uses bigint: full 4-limb square via b_mant * b_mant; track the bit-length to decide on the shift; extract rb/sb. The C function is static; we grade through public mpfr_sqr with prec(b) in (64, 128).
c_signature: static int mpfr_sqr_2(mpfr_ptr a, mpfr_srcptr b, mpfr_rnd_t rnd_mode, mpfr_prec_t p)
divergence_from_c: Same divergences as sqr_1 (output Result; sign always +; implicit prec equality b.prec == prec). Additionally: the C source uses an approximate-rounding fast path with a fallback to the full computation when the approximation is insufficient; the TS port can just always do the full bigint square (no perf benefit to the fast path in bigint land). Both produce the same value/ternary. The overflow check delegates to mpfr_overflow; underflow is omitted.
refs: mpfr/src/sqr.c L232-L356 — the C reference body (64<prec<=128 case).
mpfr/src/sqr.c L545-L546 — dispatcher routes GMP_NUMB_BITS<prec<2*GMP_NUMB_BITS here.
src/ops/add1sp2.ts / mpfr_add1sp2 spec — two-limb fast-path layout analog.
CLAUDE.md 'Hallucination-risk callouts: umul_ppmm output args are first'.
